title: Retrieve and Use Mass Spectrometry Data from Metabolomics Workbench
text: Metabolomics experiments results including mass spectrometry (MS) data can deposited several public repositories, Metabolomics Workbench repository, data resource developed NIH Common Fund’s Data Repository Coordinating Center (DRCC) San Diego Supercomputer Center, University California San Diego. data available, manual lookup download cumbersome hampering re-analysis public data replication results. MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench package closes gap providing functionality query, retrieve cache MS data Metabolomics Workbench directly R hence enabling direct seamless integration MS data Metabolomics Workbench R-based analysis workflows. MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench leverages Bioconductor’s BiocFileCache caching remote data locally provides MS data backend Spectra package enable loading integrating cached MS data directly R.

title: Retrieve and Use Mass Spectrometry Data from Metabolomics Workbench
text: package can installed within R commands :

what: Importing MS Data from Metabolomics Workbench
title: Retrieve and Use Mass Spectrometry Data from Metabolomics Workbench
text: experiment Metabolomics Workbench identified unique accession starting ST followed number. repository provides programmatic access via Metabolomics Workbench REST API POST requests, users can query experiments associated data files directly. MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench, can resolve accessions download supported MS files (mzML/CDF/mzXML) local cache, load Spectra object downstream processing. list files Metabolomics Workbench experiment ST002115. MS data files supported formats (mzML, CDF, mzXML) can directly loaded using MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench backend R Spectra object (MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench directly extends Spectra’s MsBackendMzR backend therefore supports import MS data files formats). two supported download modes: POST request fetch individual data files. (default) FTP request download zip containing files experiment. list zip file Metabolomics Workbench experiment ST002115. FTP archive contains files experiment, may include unneeded files. subset files needed, default POST option (ftp_zip = FALSE) efficient. default, MS data files data set retrieved, example restrict data files reduce amount data needs downloaded. end define pattern matching file name data files using filePattern parameter. call downloaded 4 files experiment local cache loaded Spectra object. downloading caching data handled Bioconductor’s BiocFileCache. local cache can thus also managed directly using functionality package. subsequent loading data files load locally cached versions avoiding thus repetitive download data. Spectra object MS data files Metabolomics Workbench data set enables now subsequent analysis data R. top spectra variables mass peak data values provided MS data files also additional information related Metabolomics Workbench data set available specific spectra variables. list available spectra variables data set . Metabolomics Workbench-specific variables "mwb_id", "zip_file" "file_name" providing Metabolomics Workbench ID data set, zip file name FTP server original data file name Metabolomics Workbench individual spectrum. mwb_sync() function can used synchronize local content MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench useful , example, locally cached files deleted. function checks data files backend available locally eventually downloads caches missing files. addition, also possible manually cache download selected files Metabolomics Workbench using mwb_sync_data_files() function. downloading, function first evaluates respective data files already cached downloads needed. result, function returns data.frame storage location information cached file(s). use function retrieve local storage information one data files Metabolomics Workbench data set ST002115: mwb_cached_data_files() function can used inspect list locally cached Metabolomics Workbench data files. function require active internet connection since local content queried. default settings, data.frame available data files returned. Locally cached files Metabolomics Workbench data set can removed using mwb_delete_cache() function providing ID Metabolomics Workbench data set local data files removed.
code: library(MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench)  #' List files of a Metabolomics Workbench data set all_files <- mwb_list_files("ST002115") head(all_files) ##             zip_file              sample_file ## 1  ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML ## 3  ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_02_RP.mzXML ## 5  ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_03_RP.mzXML ## 7  ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_01_RP.mzXML ## 9  ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_02_RP.mzXML ## 11 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_03_RP.mzXML #' List zipped FTP files for a Metabolomics Workbench data set mwb_ftp_list_files("ST002115") ## [1] "ST002115_Data.zip" library(Spectra)  #' Load MS data files of one data set s <- Spectra("ST002115", filePattern = "01_RP.mzXML$", ftp_zip = FALSE,              source = MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench()) s ## MSn data (Spectra) with 5751 spectra in a MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench backend: ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            0  0.569753         1 ## 2            0  1.627010         2 ## 3            0  2.684380         3 ## 4            0  3.739880         4 ## 5            0  4.795500         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 5747         0   1498.87      1435 ## 5748         0   1499.93      1436 ## 5749         0   1500.98      1437 ## 5750         0   1502.04      1438 ## 5751         0   1503.10      1439 ##  ... 37 more variables/columns. ##  ## file(s): ## ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML ## ST002115_Data_HT1080_ML162_01_RP.mzXML ## ST002115_Data_HT1080_ML210_01_RP.mzXML ##  ... 1 more files spectraVariables(s) ##  [1] "msLevel"                  "rtime"                    ##  [3] "acquisitionNum"           "scanIndex"                ##  [5] "dataStorage"              "dataOrigin"               ##  [7] "centroided"               "smoothed"                 ##  [9] "polarity"                 "precScanNum"              ## [11] "precursorMz"              "precursorIntensity"       ## [13] "precursorCharge"          "collisionEnergy"          ## [15] "isolationWindowLowerMz"   "isolationWindowTargetMz"  ## [17] "isolationWindowUpperMz"   "peaksCount"               ## [19] "totIonCurrent"            "basePeakMZ"               ## [21] "basePeakIntensity"        "electronBeamEnergy"       ## [23] "ionisationEnergy"         "lowMZ"                    ## [25] "highMZ"                   "mergedScan"               ## [27] "mergedResultScanNum"      "mergedResultStartScanNum" ## [29] "mergedResultEndScanNum"   "injectionTime"            ## [31] "filterString"             "spectrumId"               ## [33] "ionMobilityDriftTime"     "scanWindowLowerLimit"     ## [35] "scanWindowUpperLimit"     "mwb_id"                   ## [37] "zip_file"                 "file_name" spectraData(s, c("mwb_id", "zip_file", "file_name")) ## DataFrame with 5751 rows and 3 columns ##           mwb_id          zip_file              file_name ##      <character>       <character>            <character> ## 1       ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 2       ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 3       ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 4       ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 5       ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## ...          ...               ...                    ... ## 5747    ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 5748    ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 5749    ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 5750    ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. ## 5751    ST002115 ST002115_Data.zip ST002115_Data_HT1080.. basename(s$file_name) |> head() ## [1] "ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML" ## [2] "ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML" ## [3] "ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML" ## [4] "ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML" ## [5] "ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML" ## [6] "ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML" mwb_sync(s@backend) ## MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench with 5751 spectra ##        msLevel     rtime scanIndex ##      <integer> <numeric> <integer> ## 1            0  0.569753         1 ## 2            0  1.627010         2 ## 3            0  2.684380         3 ## 4            0  3.739880         4 ## 5            0  4.795500         5 ## ...        ...       ...       ... ## 5747         0   1498.87      1435 ## ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML ## ST002115_Data_HT1080_ML162_01_RP.mzXML ## ST002115_Data_HT1080_ML210_01_RP.mzXML ##  ... 1 more files res <- mwb_sync_data_files("ST002115",                             fileName = "HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML") res ## what: General use and information retrieval from Metabolomics Workbench
title: Retrieve and Use Mass Spectrometry Data from Metabolomics Workbench
text: Next MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench backend Spectra objects, MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench package provides also various utility functions query retrieve information Metabolomics Workbench. mwb_rest_request() queries Metabolomics Workbench REST API given study/analysis ID output item (e.g. summary, factors). Returns raw response character string format specified outputFormat (json txt). query REST API summary Metabolomics Workbench data set ST002115: mwb_ftp_download() function allows download zip archive experiment (directly, .e., without caching). example download zip archive temporary folder. example run reduce amount data needs downloaded. mwb_metadata() function retrieves metadata given Metabolomics Workbench data set list 2 data.frame: - MS_run: contains metadata MS runs data set, identified analysis ID(s), - sample_annotation: contains metadata samples data set. experiments column associated sample file name, association cab retrieved mwb_list_files() function. function handles case multiple analysis IDs combining metadata analysis IDs single data.frame experiment single data.frame sample annotation. retrieve metadata data set ST002115:
code: summary <- mwb_rest_request("ST002115", outputItem = "summary",                             outputFormat = "json") fromJSON(summary) ## $study_id ## [1] "ST002115" ##  ## $study_title ## [1] "LC-MS analysis of metabolic changes induced by GPX4 inhibitor treatment in cultured HT1080 cells" ##  ## $species ## [1] "Homo sapiens" ##  ## $institute ## [1] "University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center" ##  ## $analysis_type ## [1] "LC-MS" ##  ## $number_of_samples ## [1] "12" ##  ## $submission_date ## [1] "2022-04-20" ##  ## $release_date ## [1] "2022-04-14" ##  ## $version ## [1] "1" ##  ## $revision_no ## [1] "1" ##  ## $revision_datetime ## [1] "-" ##  ## $revision_comment ## [1] "-" ##  ## $license ## [1] "CC BY 4.0" ##  ## $license_url ## [1] "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/" ##  ## $study_url ## [1] "https://www.metabolomicsworkbench.org/data/DRCCMetadata.php?StudyID=ST002115" mwb_ftp_download("ST002115", path = tempdir()) meta <- mwb_metadata("ST002115") meta$MS_run ##   STUDY_ID ANALYSIS_ID VERSION CREATED_ON ## 1 ST002115    AN003513       1 02-08-2024 ## 2 ST002115    AN003514       1 02-08-2024 ##                                                                                      PROJECT_TITLE ## 1 A ferroptosis defense mechanism mediated by glycerol 3-phosphate dehydrogenase 2 in mitochondria ## 2 A ferroptosis defense mechanism mediated by glycerol 3-phosphate dehydrogenase 2 in mitochondria ##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    PROJECT_SUMMARY ## 1 Mechanisms of defense against ferroptosis (an iron-dependent form of cell death induced by lipid peroxidation) in cellular organelles remain poorly understood, hindering our ability to target ferroptosis in disease treatment. In this study, metabolomic analyses revealed that treatment of cancer cells with glutathione peroxidase 4 (GPX4) inhibitors results in intracellular glycerol 3-phosphate (G3P) depletion. We further showed that supplementation of cancer cells with G3P attenuates ferroptosis induced by GPX4 inhibitors in a G3P dehydrogenase 2 (GPD2)-dependent manner; GPD2 deletion sensitizes cancer cells to GPX4 inhibition-induced mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and ferroptosis, and combined deletion of GPX4 and GPD2 synergistically suppresses tumor growth by inducing ferroptosis in vivo. Mechanistically, inner mitochondrial membrane-localized GPD2 couples G3P oxidation with ubiquinone reduction to ubiquinol, which acts as a radical-trapping antioxidant to suppress ferroptosis in mitochondria. Taken together, these results reveal that GPD2 participates in ferroptosis defense in mitochondria by generating ubiquinol. ## 2 Mechanisms of defense against ferroptosis (an iron-dependent form of cell death induced by lipid peroxidation) in cellular organelles remain poorly understood, hindering our ability to target ferroptosis in disease treatment. In this study, metabolomic analyses revealed that treatment of cancer cells with glutathione peroxidase 4 (GPX4) inhibitors results in intracellular glycerol 3-phosphate (G3P) depletion. We further showed that supplementation of cancer cells with G3P attenuates ferroptosis induced by GPX4 inhibitors in a G3P dehydrogenase 2 (GPD2)-dependent manner; GPD2 deletion sensitizes cancer cells to GPX4 inhibition-induced mitochondrial lipid peroxidation and ferroptosis, and combined deletion of GPX4 and GPD2 synergistically suppresses tumor growth by inducing ferroptosis in vivo. Mechanistically, inner mitochondrial membrane-localized GPD2 couples G3P oxidation with ubiquinone reduction to ubiquinol, which acts as a radical-trapping antioxidant to suppress ferroptosis in mitochondria. Taken together, these results reveal that GPD2 participates in ferroptosis defense in mitochondria by generating ubiquinol. ##                                       INSTITUTE LAST_NAME FIRST_NAME ## 1 University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center       Gan       Boyi ## 2 University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center       Gan       Boyi ##                                    ADDRESS               EMAIL        PHONE ## 1 6565 MD Anderson Blvd, Houston, TX 77030 bgan@mdanderson.org 713-792-8653 ## 2 6565 MD Anderson Blvd, Houston, TX 77030 bgan@mdanderson.org 713-792-8653 ##                                 DOI ## 1 http://dx.doi.org/10.21228/M8HD8Q ## 2 http://dx.doi.org/10.21228/M8HD8Q ##                                                                                        STUDY_TITLE ## 1 LC-MS analysis of metabolic changes induced by GPX4 inhibitor treatment in cultured HT1080 cells ## 2 LC-MS analysis of metabolic changes induced by GPX4 inhibitor treatment in cultured HT1080 cells ##                                                                                                                                                                                       STUDY_SUMMARY ## 1 HT1080 cells were treated with vehicle (DMSO), RSL3 (10 micromolar), ML210 (10 micromolar), or ML162 (10 micromolar) for 2 hours. Cellular metabolites were then extracted and analyzed by LC-MS. ## 2 HT1080 cells were treated with vehicle (DMSO), RSL3 (10 micromolar), ML210 (10 micromolar), or ML162 (10 micromolar) for 2 hours. Cellular metabolites were then extracted and analyzed by LC-MS. ##   SUBMIT_DATE   SUBJECT_TYPE SUBJECT_SPECIES TAXONOMY_ID CELL_STRAIN_DETAILS ## 1  2022-03-02 Cultured cells    Homo sapiens        9606              HT1080 ## 2  2022-03-02 Cultured cells    Homo sapiens        9606              HT1080 ##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   COLLECTION_SUMMARY ## 1 Metabolites were extracted from cells in 35 mm culture plates by rapidly aspirating the culture medium and incubating the plates with 0.6 ml of an 80% methanol: 20% water mixture on a cold block on dry ice for 15 min. Next, the cell material was scraped into Eppendorf tubes pre-chilled on ice. After centrifugation at 13,000 RCF for 5 min at 4 °C, the supernatant was collected into a fresh tube and stored on dry ice until analysis. ## 2 Metabolites were extracted from cells in 35 mm culture plates by rapidly aspirating the culture medium and incubating the plates with 0.6 ml of an 80% methanol: 20% water mixture on a cold block on dry ice for 15 min. Next, the cell material was scraped into Eppendorf tubes pre-chilled on ice. After centrifugation at 13,000 RCF for 5 min at 4 °C, the supernatant was collected into a fresh tube and stored on dry ice until analysis. ##      SAMPLE_TYPE STORAGE_CONDITIONS ## 1 Cultured cells               -80? ## 2 Cultured cells               -80? ##                                                                                                                                TREATMENT_SUMMARY ## 1 Cells were seeded in 35-mm culture plates. When the cell confluence reached 70-80%, cells were treated with RSL3, ML210, or ML162 for 2 hours. ## 2 Cells were seeded in 35-mm culture plates. When the cell confluence reached 70-80%, cells were treated with RSL3, ML210, or ML162 for 2 hours. ##                                                                                                                                                                                                                              SAMPLEPREP_SUMMARY ## 1 For analysis by reverse phase chromatography, just before analysis, 500 µL of extract was dried under a nitrogen gas flow and then resuspended in 100 µL of water. For analysis by HILIC chromatography, the extracts were analyzed directly. ## 2 For analysis by reverse phase chromatography, just before analysis, 500 µL of extract was dried under a nitrogen gas flow and then resuspended in 100 µL of water. For analysis by HILIC chromatography, the extracts were analyzed directly. ##   PROCESSING_STORAGE_CONDITIONS EXTRACT_STORAGE ## 1                            4?              4? ## 2                            4?              4? ##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  CHROMATOGRAPHY_SUMMARY ## 1 The gradient was 0 min, 0% B; 2.5 min, 0% B; 5 min, 20% B; 7.5 min, 20% B; 13 min, 55% B; 15.5 min, 95% B; 18.5 min, 95% B; 19 min, 0% B; and 25 min, 0% B. Solvent A was 10 mM tributylamine and 15 mm acetic acid in water; Solvent B was methanol. The injection volume was 10 µL. ## 2 The gradient was 0 min, 85% B; 2 min, 85% B; 3 min, 80% B; 5 min, 80% B; 6 min, 75% B; 7 min, 75% B; 8 min, 70% B; 9 min, 70% B; 10 min, 50% B; 12 min, 50% B; 13 min, 25% B; 16 min, 25% B; 18 min, 0% B; 23 min, 0% B; 24 min, 85% B; 30 min, 85% B. The injection volume was 5 µL. ##      INSTRUMENT_NAME                                         COLUMN_NAME ## 1 Thermo Accela 1250 Phenomenex Synergi Hydro RP 100 A (100 x 2mm,2.5um) ## 2 Thermo Accela 1250   Waters XBridge BEH Amide (150 x 2.1mm,2.5um,100A) ##   COLUMN_TEMPERATURE FLOW_RATE ## 1                 40       200 ## 2                 40       150 ##                                                   SOLVENT_A         SOLVENT_B ## 1        100% water; 15 mM acetic acid; 10 mM tributylamine     100% methanol ## 2 95% water/5% acetonitrile; 20 mM ammonium acetate, pH 9.4 100% acetonitrile ##   CHROMATOGRAPHY_TYPE ANALYSIS_TYPE INSTRUMENT_TYPE MS_TYPE ## 1      Reversed phase            MS        Orbitrap     ESI ## 2               HILIC            MS        Orbitrap     ESI ##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   MS_COMMENTS ## 1 The scan range was 80-1000 m/z. Raw data files were converted to mzXML format using msconvert (ProteoWizard). Data was analyzed in the MAVEN software suite and metabolite assignments were made using a previously generated list of retention times derived from pure standard solutions. ## 2 The scan range was 80-1000 m/z. Raw data files were converted to mzXML format using msconvert (ProteoWizard). Data was analyzed in the MAVEN software suite and metabolite assignments were made using a previously generated list of retention times derived from pure standard solutions. ##   ION_MODE ## 1 NEGATIVE ## 2 NEGATIVE meta$sample_annotation ##    Subject ID       Sample ID Factors: Treatment ## 1           -  HT1080_DMSO_01               DMSO ## 2           -  HT1080_DMSO_02               DMSO ## 3           -  HT1080_DMSO_03               DMSO ## 4           - HT1080_ML162_01              ML162 ## 5           - HT1080_ML162_02              ML162 ## 6           - HT1080_ML162_03              ML162 ## 7           - HT1080_ML210_01              ML210 ## 8           - HT1080_ML210_02              ML210 ## 9           - HT1080_ML210_03              ML210 ## 10          -  HT1080_RSL3_01               RSL3 ## 11          -  HT1080_RSL3_02               RSL3 ## 12          -  HT1080_RSL3_03               RSL3 ##    Additional sample data: RAW_FILE_NAME ## 1                   HT1080_DMSO_01.mzXML ## 2                   HT1080_DMSO_02.mzXML ## 3                   HT1080_DMSO_03.mzXML ## 4                  HT1080_ML162_01.mzXML ## 5                  HT1080_ML162_02.mzXML ## 6                  HT1080_ML162_03.mzXML ## 7                  HT1080_ML210_01.mzXML ## 8                  HT1080_ML210_02.mzXML ## 9                  HT1080_ML210_03.mzXML ## 10                  HT1080_RSL3_01.mzXML ## 11                  HT1080_RSL3_02.mzXML ## 12                  HT1080_RSL3_03.mzXML ##    Additional sample data: RAW_FILE_NAME_2 ## 1               HT1080_DMSO_01_HILIC.mzXML ## 2               HT1080_DMSO_02_HILIC.mzXML ## 3               HT1080_DMSO_03_HILIC.mzXML ## 4              HT1080_ML162_01_HILIC.mzXML ## 5              HT1080_ML162_02_HILIC.mzXML ## 6              HT1080_ML162_03_HILIC.mzXML ## 7              HT1080_ML210_01_HILIC.mzXML ## 8              HT1080_ML210_02_HILIC.mzXML ## 9              HT1080_ML210_03_HILIC.mzXML ## 10              HT1080_RSL3_01_HILIC.mzXML ## 11              HT1080_RSL3_02_HILIC.mzXML ## 12              HT1080_RSL3_03_HILIC.mzXML

what: Retrieve Mass Spectrometry Data from Metabolomics Workbench
title: Retrieve Mass Spectrometry Data from Metabolomics Workbench
text: repository provides backend Spectra objects represents retrieves mass spectrometry (MS) data directly data sets deposited public Metabolomics Workbench repository. Mass spectrometry data files data set downloaded cached locally using BiocFileCache package avoid repeated download.

what: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
text: Utility functions interact Metabolomics Workbench (MWB) repository, including listing, downloading, caching, querying data files study metadata. mwb_cached_data_files(): lists locally cached data files Metabolomics Workbench. Since function evaluates local content require internet connection. default parameters available data files listed. parameters can used restrict lookup. mwb_list_files(): returns available files specified Metabolomics Workbench data set submitting POST request Metabolomics Workbench archive contents endpoint. function returns data.frame columns "zip_file" "sample_file" containing archive name file name within archive. Parameter pattern allows filter results matching "sample_file" column. function requires active internet connection. mwb_rest_request(): queries Metabolomics Workbench REST API given study/analysis ID output item (e.g. "summary", "factors"). Returns raw response character string format specified outputFormat ("json" "txt"). function requires active internet connection. mwb_ftp_list_files(): queries Metabolomics Workbench FTP server given experiment ID returns related files. Parameter pattern allows filter results. contrast mwb_list_files(), function lists files FTP server (like zip file experiment), mwb_list_files() lists files contained within zip file. files may also present FTP server. function requires active internet connection. mwb_ftp_download(): download files Metabolomics Workbench FTP server given experiment ID. Use pattern filter files name using regular expression (default files downloaded). Use path set destination directory downloaded files. files listed mwb_ftp_list_files() can downloaded. mwb_metadata(): retrieves metadata given MWB data set list two data.frame: one metadata experiment one sample annotation. function handles case multiple analysis IDs combining metadata analysis IDs single data.frame experiment single data.frame sample annotation. function requires active internet connection. mwb_sync_data_files(): synchronize data files specified MWB data set eventually downloading locally caching . Parameter fileName allows specify names selected data files sync. mwb_delete_cache(): removes local content mwb data set ID mwbId. delete eventually present locally cached data files specified data set. change data eventually present local BiocFileCache.

title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
code: mwb_list_files(x = character(), pattern = NULL)  mwb_rest_request(   id = character(),   idType = c("study_id", "analysis_id"),   outputItem = character(),   outputFormat = c("json", "txt") )  mwb_ftp_list_files(mwbId = character(), pattern = "*")  mwb_ftp_download(   mwbId = character(),   pattern = "*",   path = "./",   overwrite = FALSE )  mwb_metadata(mwbId = character())  mwb_sync_data_files(   mwbId = character(),   pattern = "mzML$|mzml$|CDF$|cdf$|mzXML$",   fileName = character(),   ftp_zip = FALSE )  mwb_cached_data_files(   mwbId = character(),   pattern = "*",   fileName = character() )  mwb_delete_cache(mwbId = character())

title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
text: x character(1) ID MBW data set (usually starting ST followed number). pattern mwb_list_files(), mwb_sync_data_files(), mwb_cached_data_files(), mwb_ftp_list_files mwb_ftp_download: character(1) defining pattern filter file names, pattern = "mzML$" retrieve file names files data set (.e., files extension "mzML"). parameter passed grepl() function. id character(1) ID single Metabolomics Workbench data set/experiment. idType mwb_rest_request(): character(1) defining type ID provided id. accepted ID types "study_id" "analysis_id". default "study_id". outputItem mwb_rest_request(): character(1) defining metadata retrieve Metabolomics Workbench. get information possible output visit webpage MBW REST API. outputFormat mwb_rest_request(): character(1) defining output format metadata. supported output json txt. mwbId character(1) ID single Metabolomics Workbench data set/experiment. path mwb_ftp_download(): optional character defining directory download files. overwrite mwb_ftp_download(): logical(1) whether existing files overwritten. Defaults FALSE, case files already exist path skipped. fileName mwb_sync_data_files() mwb_cached_data_files(): optional character defining names specific data files data set downloaded cached. ftp_zip mwb_sync_data_files(): logical(1) download complete zip experiment FTP server. Defaults FALSE, case files downloaded singularly via POST request.

title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
text: mwb_list_files(): data.frame columns zip_file sample_file containing, respectively, archive name relative file within archive mwb_rest_request(): character(1) raw REST API response body, formatted JSON plain text depending outputFormat. mwb_sync_data_files() mwb_cached_data_files(): data.frame MWB ID, name(s) remote local file names synchronized data files. mwb_ftp_list_files: character files FTP server specific ID. mwb_metadata: list two data.frame: one metadata experiment one sample annotation.

title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
text: Metabolomics Workbench provides metadata REST API. MS data files can obtained two ways: Downloading full zip archive FTP server. POST request MWB archive page gets correct zip archive name MWB ID. archive contains files experiment, may include also unneeded files. subset files needed, second option efficient. Download individual files using two-step POST-based procedure: query MWB archive page get exact file names. , download file via POST request.

title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
text: Gabriele Tomè, Johannes Rainer, Philippine Louail

title: Utility functions for the Metabolomics Workbench repository — MetabolomicsWorkbench-utils
code: ## Retrieve available files for the data set ST002115 mwb_list_files("ST002115") #>             zip_file                 sample_file #> 1  ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML #> 3  ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_DMSO_02_RP.mzXML #> 5  ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_DMSO_03_RP.mzXML #> 7  ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML162_01_RP.mzXML #> 9  ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML162_02_RP.mzXML #> 11 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML162_03_RP.mzXML #> 13 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML210_01_RP.mzXML #> 15 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML210_02_RP.mzXML #> 17 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML210_03_RP.mzXML #> 19 ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_RSL3_01_RP.mzXML #> 21 ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_RSL3_02_RP.mzXML #> 23 ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_RSL3_03_RP.mzXML #> 25 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 27 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 29 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_03_HILIC.mzXML #> 31 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 33 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 35 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_03_HILIC.mzXML #> 37 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML210_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 39 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML210_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 41 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML210_03_HILIC.mzXML #> 43 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_RSL3_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 45 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_RSL3_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 47 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_RSL3_03_HILIC.mzXML  ## Retrieve the available .mzML files. A1_mzMLfiles <- mwb_list_files("ST000016", pattern = "A1") A1_mzMLfiles #>              zip_file                          sample_file #> 233 ST000016_mzML.zip D20101022-LC2-PP0000705-A1-I1-P.mzML #> 235 ST000016_mzML.zip D20101022-LC2-PP0000705-A1-I1-N.mzML #> 485 ST000016_mzML.zip D20101020-LC2-PP0000698-A1-I1-Z.mzML #> 487 ST000016_mzML.zip D20101020-LC2-PP0000698-A1-I1-P.mzML #> 489 ST000016_mzML.zip D20101020-LC2-PP0000698-A1-I1-N.mzML #> 491 ST000016_mzML.zip D20101025-LC2-PP0000705-A1-I2-Z.mzML  ## Query the REST API for a study summary in JSON format mwb_rest_request("ST002115", outputItem = "summary") #> [1] "{\"study_id\":\"ST002115\",\"study_title\":\"LC-MS analysis of metabolic changes induced by GPX4 inhibitor treatment in cultured HT1080 cells\",\"species\":\"Homo sapiens\",\"institute\":\"University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center\",\"analysis_type\":\"LC-MS\",\"number_of_samples\":\"12\",\"submission_date\":\"2022-04-20\",\"release_date\":\"2022-04-14\",\"version\":\"1\",\"revision_no\":\"1\",\"revision_datetime\":\"-\",\"revision_comment\":\"-\",\"license\":\"CC BY 4.0\",\"license_url\":\"https:\\/\\/creativecommons.org\\/licenses\\/by\\/4.0\\/\",\"study_url\":\"https:\\/\\/www.metabolomicsworkbench.org\\/data\\/DRCCMetadata.php?StudyID=ST002115\"}"  ## List zip file of the data set ST002115 mwb_ftp_list_files("ST002115") #> [1] "ST002115_Data.zip"  ## Download the file with: `mwb_ftp_download("ST002115", path = tempdir())`

what: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
text: MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench retrieves represents mass spectrometry (MS) data metabolomics studies stored Metabolomics Workbench repository, data resource developed NIH Common Fund's Data Repository Coordinating Center (DRCC) San Diego Supercomputer Center, University California San Diego. repository provides access study metadata, processed experimental results, metabolite structures, reference compound information RESTful HTTP API / FTP server / POST request. backend directly extends Spectra::MsBackendMzR backend Spectra package hence supports MS data mzML, CDF, mzXML format. Data formats loaded MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench. Upon initialization backendInitialize() method, MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench backend fetches caches study data files locally using Bioconductor's BiocFileCache package, avoiding repeated queries remote repository. See help vignettes package details cached data resources. Additional utility functions management cached files also provided MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench. See help mwb_cached_data_files() information.

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
code: MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench()  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench' backendInitialize(   object,   mwbId = character(),   filePattern = "mzML$|CDF$|cdf$|mzXML$",   ftp_zip = FALSE,   offline = FALSE,   ... )  # S4 method for class 'MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench' backendRequiredSpectraVariables(object, ...)  mwb_sync(x, offline = FALSE)

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
text: object instance MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench. mwbId character(1) ID single MetabolomicsWorkbench data set/experiment. filePattern character pattern defining supported (requested) file types. Defaults filePattern = "mzML$|CDF$|cdf$|mzXML$" hence restricting mzML, CDF mzXML files supported Spectra's MsBackendMzR backend. ftp_zip mwb_sync_data_files(): logical(1) download complete zip experiment FTP server. Defaults FALSE, case files downloaded singularly via POST request. offline logical(1) whether locally cached content evaluated/loaded. ... additional parameters; currently ignored. x instance MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench.

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
text: MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench(): instance MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench. backendInitialize(): instance MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench MS data specified MetabolomicsWorkbench data set. backendRequiredSpectraVariables(): character spectra variables needed backend provide MS data. mwb_sync(): input MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench paths locally cached data files eventually updated.

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
text: backend uses BiocFileCache package caching data files. stored default local BiocFileCache cache along additional metadata includes Metabolomics Workbench ID. Note present MS data files mzML, CDF mzXML format supported. MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench backend defines provides additional spectra variables "mwb_id", "zip_file" "file_name" list MetabolomicsWorkbench ID, original zip file name original data file name Metabolomics Workbench ftp server individual spectrum. "file_name" can used mapping experiment's samples individual data files, respective spectra. MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench backend considered read-thus support changing m/z intensity values directly.

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
text: account transient network failures high server load Metabolomics Workbench endpoint, download functions automatically retry failed requests. error raised 5 consecutive failed attempts. attempt, function waits progressively increasing time period (5 seconds first second attempt, 10 seconds second third, forth). sleep time multiplier can configured via "mwb.sleep_mult" option (defaults 5). active internet connection required non-cached operations; use offline = TRUE backendInitialize() suppress remote requests rely exclusively local BiocFileCache cache.

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
text: New instances class can created MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench() function. Data loaded initialized using backendInitialize() function, accepts parameters mwbId, filePattern ftp_zip. mwbId must accession single existing Metabolomics Workbench study (e.g. "ST000016"). Optional parameter filePattern defines pattern used filter file names MS data files defaults data files file endings supported MS data formats. Optional parameter ftp_zip = TRUE download complete zip file experiment FTP server extract data files locally, can faster downloading files individually via POST request. However subset data files required, efficient download files separately via POST request ftp_zip = FALSE filePattern set desired file name pattern. backendInitialize() requires active internet connection, function queries Metabolomics Workbench via POST request compares remote file content locally cached files synchronizing changes updates. behavior can bypassed offline = TRUE, case locally cached content used. backendRequiredSpectraVariables() function returns names spectra variables required backend provide MS data. mwb_sync() function can used synchronize local data cache ensure study data files locally available. function checks local cache downloads missing data files Metabolomics Workbench repository.

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench

title: MsBackend representing MS data from Metabolomics Workbench — MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench
code: library(MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench)  ## List files of a MetabolomicsWorkbench data set mwb_list_files("ST002115") #>             zip_file                 sample_file #> 1  ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML #> 3  ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_DMSO_02_RP.mzXML #> 5  ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_DMSO_03_RP.mzXML #> 7  ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML162_01_RP.mzXML #> 9  ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML162_02_RP.mzXML #> 11 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML162_03_RP.mzXML #> 13 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML210_01_RP.mzXML #> 15 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML210_02_RP.mzXML #> 17 ST002115_Data.zip    HT1080_ML210_03_RP.mzXML #> 19 ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_RSL3_01_RP.mzXML #> 21 ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_RSL3_02_RP.mzXML #> 23 ST002115_Data.zip     HT1080_RSL3_03_RP.mzXML #> 25 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 27 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 29 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_DMSO_03_HILIC.mzXML #> 31 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 33 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 35 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML162_03_HILIC.mzXML #> 37 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML210_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 39 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML210_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 41 ST002115_Data.zip HT1080_ML210_03_HILIC.mzXML #> 43 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_RSL3_01_HILIC.mzXML #> 45 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_RSL3_02_HILIC.mzXML #> 47 ST002115_Data.zip  HT1080_RSL3_03_HILIC.mzXML  ## Initialize a MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench representing all MS ## data files of the data set with the ID "ST002115". This will ## download and cache all files and subsequently load and represent ## them in R.  be <- backendInitialize(MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench(),                         "ST002115",                         filePattern = "DMSO_01_RP.mzXML$") be #> MsBackendMetabolomicsWorkbench with 1437 spectra #>        msLevel     rtime scanIndex #>      <integer> <numeric> <integer> #> 1            0  0.569753         1 #> 2            0  1.627010         2 #> 3            0  2.684380         3 #> 4            0  3.739880         4 #> 5            0  4.795500         5 #> ...        ...       ...       ... #> 1433         0   1499.05      1433 #> 1434         0   1500.11      1434 #> 1435         0   1501.16      1435 #> 1436         0   1502.22      1436 #> 1437         0   1503.28      1437 #>  ... 37 more variables/columns. #>  #> file(s): #> ST002115_Data_HT1080_DMSO_01_RP.mzXML  ## The `mwb_sync()` function can be used to ensure that all data ## files are available locally. This function will eventually download ## missing data files or update their paths. be <- mwb_sync(be)
